name: Firebase Analytics (Measurement Protocol v2)
what_it_does: Enables server-side event tracking to Google Analytics 4 / Firebase Analytics via HTTP POST, allowing agents and backend systems to record user events and conversions without a client-side SDK.
use_cases: Track server-side conversion events (purchases, sign-ups) to GA4 when client-side tracking is blocked or unavailable
Record background job completions and batch processing outcomes as custom events in Firebase Analytics
Attribute server-rendered page views or API interactions to GA4 sessions for unified analytics
Send enriched event data with server-side user properties that are unavailable in the browser
Log agent-driven workflow events (document processed, recommendation served) for product analytics
not_for: Real-time event querying — there is no query API; data access requires BigQuery export with 24-48 hour delay
Raw data retrieval or building custom dashboards without exporting to BigQuery first
High-frequency machine telemetry logging where per-event cost and latency matter — use a purpose-built metrics store instead
best_when: You already use GA4 for analytics and need to enrich it with server-side events that can't be captured client-side, and you accept a 24-48 hour data lag.
avoid_when: You need real-time data access, SQL-queryable event logs, or low-latency analytics pipelines.

auth.notes: Measurement Protocol requires a Measurement ID (G-XXXXXXXX) and an API Secret generated in the GA4 admin. Both are passed as query parameters. No user-level auth — the API secret is a server-side secret.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Firebase Analytics and GA4 are free; BigQuery export for data access requires a GCP project (BigQuery has its own free tier: 10GB storage, 1TB queries/month)
pricing.paid_tiers: GA4 360 (enterprise) for SLA, higher limits, and advanced features; BigQuery costs scale with data volume
pricing.notes: The Measurement Protocol itself is free. Costs arise from BigQuery if exporting large event volumes for analysis.

performance.rate_limits: No officially documented rate limit for Measurement Protocol; practical limit estimated ~100,000 events/day per property before sampling kicks in

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Measurement Protocol always returns HTTP 204 No Content regardless of whether the event was accepted or silently dropped. There is no error feedback for malformed payloads in production mode — use the validation endpoint during development.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Duplicate event submissions are counted as separate events. Agents must prevent duplicate sends externally.
known_agent_gotchas: The API always returns 204 even for invalid or malformed events — use the debug endpoint (https://www.google-analytics.com/debug/mp/collect) during development to catch schema errors
Events appear in GA4 console with a 24-48 hour delay; agents should not use GA4 as a source of truth for real-time decision-making
Maximum 25 events per request and 25 parameters per event; exceeding these limits causes silent data loss
client_id and session_id must be stable and consistent across events to attribute them to sessions correctly — generating new random IDs per call fragments data
GA4 data sampling applies at high volumes on free tier; reports may not reflect exact event counts for high-traffic properties

security_score_components.security_notes: API secret is a static server-side credential with no expiry or rotation mechanism. It provides write-only access to a single GA4 property. Data sent to GA4 is subject to Google's data processing terms.
